conversation.name: Desk
conversation.statement: Yes... it's horrible. When I came to work, the lights in Dr. Phillips' office were on and I went in... and there she was, lying on the floor.
Dr. Justine Phillips joined this pain clinic not even a year ago. Such an enthusiastic young doctor.
I joined the clinic shortly after it was opened in 2005.
Dr. Brody Fowler, but he's not in yet.
Yes, she notified me about it a couple of weeks ago.
She seemed a little worn out lately. No wonder, considering the amount of patients we see here every day.
conversation.response: Can you tell me more about the victim?
How long have you worked here?
Do you know if Dr. Phillips kept a gun in her office?
How would you describe Dr. Phillips' demeanor of late?

conversation.name: House
conversation.statement: So you have some more questions about Dr. Phillips? I don't know if I can help you.
Yes, I was there. I went back to grab my cellphone, which I had left on my desk.

Only Dr. Phillips. It is not unusual for our doctors to work overtime.
And she was still very much alive, if that's what you're asking.
I don't know what you need my DNA for, but yes. I hope it's painless.

Absolutely not. I have expensive things here that I don't want people to touch.
You'll have to come back with a warrant.
conversation.response: We know that you were at the clinic around the time Dr. Phillips died.
Did you see Dr. Phillips or anybody else?
Would you be willing to provide a DNA sample?
